goal: Close automatic image classification, confidence and mode recommendation with deterministic validation contracts.

included: labeled analyzer corpus and release gates
excluded: vector candidate generation, topology and final artifact quality; closed by the core vector engine roadmap
SaaS billing, authentication, administration and user-interface work

phases.title: Finite analyzer capability and roadmap contract
acceptance_criteria: A finite four-phase analyzer roadmap is committed and schema-validated.
Automatic recommendation and explicit-only modes partition the public non-auto modes.
The public analyzer report fields and deterministic seed decisions are regression-tested.
Every known analyzer limitation has one closure phase and evidence files exist.
Production analyzer decisions remain unchanged.

phases.title: Versioned deterministic features and calibrated confidence
acceptance_criteria: Analyzer feature names, units, ranges and extraction version are machine-readable and stable.
Repeated analysis of the same decoded pixels produces identical feature and recommendation digests.
Every recommendation includes bounded confidence and runner-up margin derived from labeled evidence.
Unavailable optional HED signals are explicit and cannot raise confidence.
Missing, non-finite, out-of-range or partial feature data produces an invalid metadata contract.

phases.title: Review-aware auto-mode decision gate
acceptance_criteria: Low-confidence or contradictory evidence produces an explicit needs_review result.
Auto mode consumes only a validated analyzer report with matching feature and source digests.
Manual explicit modes remain available and are never rewritten by confidence logic.
Boundary cases for all major image classes have deterministic review regressions.
Missing confidence, stale metadata, digest drift and unsupported recommendations are rejected.

phases.title: Labeled analyzer corpus and release closure
acceptance_criteria: Every automatic recommendation mode has labeled in-domain and boundary-case corpus coverage.
Three-run corpus results are deterministic and contain confusion, calibration and review metrics.
Per-mode precision, high-impact classification-error and confidence-calibration thresholds are explicit.
Optional-backend and no-HED environments produce documented deterministic results.
Analyzer contract, corpus and benchmark workflows are mandatory and green.
